2026-06-08.content: The workstation configuration creation page in the Google Cloud console has been optimized to make configuration creation faster and easier. Common machine settings are grouped into selectable machine presets, frequently used settings are consolidated on the **Configuration essentials** landing page, and a pending cluster with default settings is automatically provisioned when no cluster exists in your selected region.

summary.title: Streamlined Workstation Configuration
summary.summary: The Google Cloud console now offers an optimized workstation configuration page for faster and easier setup. New features include selectable machine presets, consolidated essential settings, and automatic cluster provisioning to simplify your workflow.

summary.title: New Preconfigured Base Images Available
summary.summary: We're excited to announce the release of new preconfigured base images for workstations. These images now include the Antigravity CLI, offering enhanced development capabilities.

2026-05-29.content:
Cloud Workstations supports configuring custom startup scripts from the Google Cloud Console.
When you create or edit a workstation configuration, you can specify the Cloud Storage path to your startup script under the advanced environment customization options. For more information, see [Run custom startup scripts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/startup-scripts).

summary.title: Configure custom startup scripts
summary.summary: You can now configure custom startup scripts directly from the Google Cloud Console when creating or editing workstation configurations. This allows for more flexible environment customization by specifying the Cloud Storage path to your script under advanced options.

2026-05-29.content:
The base VM was upgraded to use [Container-Optimized OS](https://docs.cloud.google.com/container-optimized-os/docs/concepts/features-and-benefits)
[129 LTS](https://docs.cloud.google.com/container-optimized-os/docs/release-notes/m129).

summary.title: New OS version for VMs
summary.summary: The base VM image has been updated to the latest Container-Optimized OS version, 129 LTS. This upgrade brings the newest features and security patches to your virtual machines.

2026-05-29.content:
The [JetBrains RubyMine preconfigured base image](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/preconfigured-base-images#list_of_preconfigured_base_images)
uses a custom gem directory (`/usr/local/share/gems/ruby/3.1.0`).

summary.title: RubyMine base image update
summary.summary: The RubyMine preconfigured base image now uses a custom gem directory, specifically /usr/local/share/gems/ruby/3.1.0. This change ensures compatibility and proper management of Ruby gems within the environment.

2026-05-18.content:
Cloud Workstations supports resizing workstations' persistent directories.
For more information, see [Resize workstation persistent directories](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/resize-workstation-persistent-directories).

summary.title: Resize persistent directories in Cloud Workstations
summary.summary: You can now resize persistent directories for your Cloud Workstations. This feature allows you to adjust the storage capacity of your workstation's persistent disk to better suit your needs. For more details, refer to the documentation on resizing workstation persistent directories.

2026-05-10.content:
The base VM was upgraded to use [Container-Optimized OS](https://docs.cloud.google.com/container-optimized-os/docs/concepts/features-and-benefits)
[125 LTS](https://docs.cloud.google.com/container-optimized-os/docs/release-notes/m125).

summary.title: VM image updated to COS 125 LTS
summary.summary: The base VM image has been upgraded to use Container-Optimized OS (COS) version 125 LTS. This update brings the latest features and security enhancements to the operating system. The upgrade ensures a more robust and efficient environment for running containerized applications.

2026-05-08.content:
You can configure a workstation authorization URL for workstation clusters.

When you specify an authorization URL, unauthorized HTTP or HTTPS requests received
by workstation VMs in the cluster are redirected to this endpoint. The endpoint
is then responsible for retrieving an access token and redirecting back to the
original hostname with the token.

summary.title: Configure workstation authorization URL
summary.summary: You can now configure a workstation authorization URL for workstation clusters. When specified, unauthorized requests to workstation VMs will be redirected to this endpoint to retrieve an access token and redirect back to the original hostname.

2026-05-04.content:
The [JetBrains Rider preconfigured base image](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/preconfigured-base-images#list_of_preconfigured_base_images)
includes .NET 10.0 and drops .NET 6.0.

summary.title: JetBrains Rider Base Image Update
summary.summary: The JetBrains Rider preconfigured base image now includes .NET 10.0 and has removed support for .NET 6.0. This update ensures you have the latest .NET framework for your development environment.

2026-05-04.content:
The [JetBrains RubyMine preconfigured base image](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/preconfigured-base-images#list_of_preconfigured_base_images)
installs Ruby using `ruby-build` instead of RVM (Ruby Version Manager).

summary.title: New RubyMine Image Available
summary.summary: A new preconfigured base image for JetBrains RubyMine is now available. This image installs Ruby using ruby-build, offering an alternative to RVM.

2026-04-27.content:
Cloud Workstations UI supports additional machine series and types, including
**A2**, **A3**, **C3**, **C4**, **G4**, **M3**, **N4**, and **Z3**.
You can also select **Confidential C3 standard** machine types. For more
information, see [Available machine types](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/available-machine-types).

The [preconfigured base images](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/preconfigured-base-images)
include a notification when the `running_timeout` for the workstation is
close to being reached.

summary.title: New machine types and notifications in Cloud Workstations
summary.summary: Cloud Workstations now supports a wider range of machine types, including A2, A3, C3, C4, G4, M3, N4, and Z3, as well as Confidential C3 standard types. Additionally, preconfigured base images now include notifications when a workstation's running timeout is approaching.

2026-04-13.content:
The [preconfigured base images](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/preconfigured-base-images)
include a `/google/scripts/keep_alive.sh` script to prevent idle
timeouts when running long-running background tasks.

summary.title: Prevent idle timeouts for background tasks
summary.summary: New preconfigured base images now include a keep-alive script. This script helps prevent idle timeouts when running long-running background tasks.

2026-04-06.content:
You can automatically redirect and restart stopped workstations by configuring
the `workstation_launch_url` Workstation cluster field. To set a custom URL or
use the built-in Google Cloud Console launcher,
[update your cluster](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/create-cluster#workstation-cluster).

summary.title: Automate Workstation Restarts
summary.summary: You can now automatically redirect and restart stopped workstations by configuring the workstation_launch_url cluster field. This allows for custom URLs or the use of the built-in Google Cloud Console launcher to manage your workstation lifecycle.

2026-03-06.content:
The [Cloud Workstations base editor](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/preconfigured-ides#cloud-workstations-base-editor)
(Code OSS for Cloud Workstations) has been upgraded to `1.108.2`. The last image that offers the
previous version is tagged `code-oss-1.104.3`.

summary.title: Base editor updated
summary.summary: The Cloud Workstations base editor has been upgraded to version 1.108.2. The previous version is available under the tag code-oss-1.104.3.

2026-02-19.content:
You can configure [Hyperdisk Balanced High Availability](https://docs.cloud.google.com/compute/docs/disks/hyperdisks)
for persistent directories directly in the Google Cloud console.
This option is available in the **Disk type** menu when you create or
update workstation configurations for supported machine series.

Additionally, [C3 machine types](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/available-machine-types)
are available for selection in the Google Cloud console. For the
C3 machine series, which only supports Hyperdisk, the console automatically
selects Hyperdisk as the disk type.

summary.title: Configure Hyperdisk Balanced HA for Workstations
summary.summary: You can now configure Hyperdisk Balanced High Availability for persistent disks directly in the Google Cloud console. This feature is available when creating or updating workstation configurations. Additionally, C3 machine types are now available, with Hyperdisk automatically selected for this series.

2026-02-18.content:
Clicking **Launch** for a workstation in the Google Cloud console opens a page
that automatically starts the workstation and connects to it. You can bookmark
this page to restart and access your workstation. To start a workstation
without connecting, select **Start** in the **Launch** drop-down menu.

summary.title: Start and connect to your workstation
summary.summary: You can now launch and connect to your workstation directly from the Google Cloud console. Bookmark the launch page to easily restart and access your workstation. Alternatively, use the 'Start' option in the launch menu to begin a session without connecting.

2026-02-06.content:
You can provide [custom metadata](https://docs.cloud.google.com/compute/docs/metadata/overview) for a
workstation's host VM.

Use the `--instance-metadata` flag with the
[`gcloud workstations configs create`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/workstations/configs/create)
and [`gcloud workstations configs update`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/workstations/configs/update)
commands. Format the metadata as a comma-separated list of key-value pairs—for
example, `--instance-metadata=key1=value1,key2=value2`.

Note that updating metadata replaces all existing custom metadata for the
workstation instance.

summary.title: Add custom metadata to workstation VMs
summary.summary: You can now provide custom metadata for your workstation's host VMs. Use the --instance-metadata flag with the gcloud workstations configs create and update commands to set key-value pairs. Note that updating metadata will replace all existing custom metadata.

2026-02-06.content:
Cloud Workstations supports specifying a custom startup script to be executed on
the workstation's host VM during boot.

Provide the custom startup script as a Google Cloud Storage URL, such as
`gs://BUCKET/FILE`, using the `--startup-script-uri` flag with the
[`gcloud workstations configs create`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/workstations/configs/create) and [`gcloud workstations configs update`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/workstations/configs/update)
commands.

summary.title: Custom startup scripts for Cloud Workstations
summary.summary: You can now specify a custom startup script to run when your Cloud Workstation's host VM boots. Provide the script as a Google Cloud Storage URL using the --startup-script-uri flag with gcloud workstations configs create and update commands.

2026-01-31.content:
Cloud Workstations supports [Hyperdisk Balanced High Availability](https://docs.cloud.google.com/compute/docs/disks/hyperdisks) (`hyperdisk-balanced-ha`) for persistent directories on configurations using A3, C3, C4, G4, M3, N4, N4D, and Z3 machine series. You can specify the disk type using the `--disk-type` flag with the [`gcloud workstations configs create`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/workstations/configs/create) and [`gcloud workstations configs update`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/workstations/configs/update) commands.

Cloud Workstations supports A3 machine types with Hyperdisk only. For more details, see [Available machine types](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/available-machine-types) and [Available GPUs](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/available-gpus).

summary.title: Cloud Workstations adds Hyperdisk Balanced HA support
summary.summary: Cloud Workstations now supports Hyperdisk Balanced High Availability for persistent directories on select machine types, including A3, C3, C4, G4, M3, N4, N4D, and Z3. This enhancement improves data durability and availability for your development environments. You can configure this new disk type using the `--disk-type` flag in gcloud commands.

2025-12-05.content:
Cloud Workstations predefined images include
[Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) 24 (LTS). The previous version, 20.19.6, is no
longer pre-installed. To continue using a previous version, you can
[customize your container images](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/customize-container-images)
to include it or add a version manager like [NVM](https://www.nvmnode.com/).

summary.title: Node.js 24 now available in Cloud Workstations
summary.summary: Cloud Workstations now includes Node.js 24 (LTS) as a predefined image, replacing version 20.19.6. If you need to use a previous version, you can customize your container images or use a version manager like NVM.

2025-12-05.content:
Cloud Workstations predefined images include
[Eclipse Temurin](https://adoptium.net/temurin) JDK 21 and 25. JDK 11 is no longer
pre-installed. To continue using JDK 11,
[customize your container images](https://docs.cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/customize-container-images).

summary.title: Cloud Workstations Image Update
summary.summary: Cloud Workstations now includes Temurin JDK 21 and 25. JDK 11 is no longer pre-installed. To keep using JDK 11, customize your container images.

2025-07-07.content: Cloud Workstations is available in the `europe-central2` region (Warsaw). For more information, see [Locations](https://cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/locations).

summary.title: Cloud Workstations now in Warsaw
summary.summary: Cloud Workstations is now accessible in the `europe-central2` region, located in Warsaw. For a comprehensive list of available regions, please refer to the documentation.

2025-06-09.content: The JetBrains [readiness server](https://cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/develop-code-using-local-jetbrains-ides#add_a_server_readiness_check) lets you configure the port it listens on and the timeout when you specify the `JETBRAINS_READY_SERVER_PORT` and `JETBRAINS_READY_SERVER_TIMEOUT` environment variables in your workstation environment. For more information about setting environment variables, see [Customizing your environment](https://cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/create-configuration#customize_environment).

summary.title: JetBrains Readiness Server Configuration
summary.summary: You can now configure the JetBrains readiness server port and timeout using environment variables. This allows for greater flexibility in customizing your development environment.

summary.title: CLion Updated to Version 2025.1.1
summary.summary: The JetBrains CLion preconfigured base image has been updated to use CLion 2025.1.1.

summary.title: GoLand Updated to 2025.1.1
summary.summary: The JetBrains GoLand preconfigured base image has been updated to use GoLand 2025.1.1. This update includes the latest features and improvements from JetBrains for Go development.

2025-06-03.content: The [JetBrains IntelliJ Ultimate preconfigured base image](https://cloud.google.com/workstations/docs/preconfigured-base-images#list_of_preconfigured_base_images) uses [IntelliJ-IDEA 2025.1.1](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/articles/IDEA-A-2100662430/IntelliJ-IDEA-2025.1.1.1-251.25410.129-build-Release-Notes)

summary.title: IntelliJ IDEA base image updated
summary.summary: The IntelliJ IDEA 2025.1.1 preconfigured base image for Cloud Workstations has been updated to the latest version.

summary.title: PhpStorm base image updated
summary.summary: The JetBrains PhpStorm preconfigured base image has been updated to PhpStorm 2025.1.1.

summary.title: WebStorm base image updated
summary.summary: The JetBrains WebStorm preconfigured base image has been updated to use WebStorm version 2025.1.1.

summary.title: RubyMine Updated
summary.summary: The JetBrains RubyMine preconfigured base image has been updated to use RubyMine 2025.1.1.

summary.title: PyCharm base image updated
summary.summary: The JetBrains PyCharm preconfigured base image has been updated to use PyCharm version 2025.1.1.1.

summary.title: Rider base image updated
summary.summary: The JetBrains Rider preconfigured base image has been updated to version 2025.1.2.
